brief_document.title: Implement dedup contract and processing receipts
brief_document.purpose: Implements the lightweight exactly-once strategy from ADR-006: a three-party dedup contract between producer (provides idempotency key), bus (maintains dedup cache), and consumer (provides processing receipt). The dedup cache maps idempotency keys to processing receipts in haematite with configurable TTL. This gives simple consumers exactly-once semantics without event-sourced processing — the complement to DUR-005's heavyweight durable conversations.
brief_document.task: Build the dedup cache and processing receipt system. The cache stores idempotency-key-to-receipt mappings in haematite. On publish, check the cache: if the key exists with a receipt, return the receipt without re-delivering; if the key exists without a receipt (in-flight), defer delivery; if the key is new, deliver and cache the key. After processing, the consumer provides a receipt that completes the cache entry. A background sweep removes entries older than the configured TTL, with a 1-second clock-skew safety margin. Scope boundary: this brief handles dedup and receipts only. It does not handle durable channel storage (DUR-002), cursors (DUR-003), or conversations (DUR-005).

requirements.title: Implement dedup cache with haematite-backed key storage
requirements.spec: DedupCache struct SHALL store idempotency key to receipt mappings in haematite via DurableStore::append(dedup_namespace + key_hash, entry_bytes, expected_seq). The cache key SHALL be derived by hashing the idempotency key. Each cache entry SHALL store: the original idempotency key, an optional processing receipt (None until consumer provides one), and the entry timestamp (epoch millis). DedupCache SHALL hold a reference to DurableStore and the dedup namespace prefix.
acceptance: DedupCache::new(store, namespace) constructs a cache with the given store and namespace.
Cache entries are stored in haematite with stream key formatted as "{namespace}:{key_hash}".
Entry serialization includes: idempotency_key, receipt (Option<Vec<u8>>), timestamp_millis.
The key hash is deterministic: same idempotency key always produces same hash.

requirements.title: Return stored receipt for duplicate publish with completed entry
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is published with an idempotency key that already exists in the dedup cache AND the cache entry has a processing receipt, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the stored receipt to the publisher without re-delivering the message. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT append the message to the durable channel. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT modify the existing cache entry.
acceptance: Publishing with a key that has a receipt returns Ok with the stored receipt bytes.
The message is not appended to the channel (no DurableStore::append call for the message).
The existing cache entry is not modified.
The returned receipt bytes are identical to what the consumer originally provided.

requirements.title: Defer delivery for duplicate publish with in-flight entry
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is published with an idempotency key that already exists in the dedup cache AND the cache entry has no processing receipt yet (processing is in-flight), THE SYSTEM SHALL defer delivery by returning a DedupInFlight status. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT re-deliver the message. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT modify the existing cache entry.
acceptance: Publishing with a key that exists but has receipt=None returns DedupInFlight status.
The message is not re-delivered to any consumer.
The existing cache entry timestamp is not updated.
The publisher receives a clear indication that the message is already being processed.

requirements.title: Implement background TTL sweep with clock-skew safety
requirements.spec: DedupSweeper SHALL periodically scan the dedup cache and remove entries whose timestamp is older than the configured TTL. The sweep SHALL subtract 1 second from the TTL threshold to account for clock skew — an entry at exactly TTL age SHALL NOT be removed. WHEN sweeping, THE SYSTEM SHALL use DurableStore::scan to enumerate entries and check timestamps. The sweep interval SHALL be configurable. TTL expiry SHALL NEVER remove an entry younger than (configured_ttl - 1 second).
acceptance: An entry with timestamp 1000ms and TTL 60000ms is not removed at time 60999ms (within TTL + 1s skew margin).
An entry with timestamp 1000ms and TTL 60000ms is removed at time 62001ms (past TTL + 1s skew margin).
Sweep does not remove entries with receipt=None (in-flight) that are within TTL.
Sweep interval is configurable, not hardcoded.
Sweep uses DurableStore::scan to enumerate, not a separate index.

requirements.title: Define ProcessingReceipt as opaque bytes
requirements.spec: ProcessingReceipt SHALL be a newtype wrapping Vec<u8> representing the opaque processing outcome provided by the consumer. ProcessingReceipt SHALL implement Debug (displaying byte length, not contents), Clone, and PartialEq. The receipt content is consumer-defined — the bus stores and returns it without interpretation.
acceptance: ProcessingReceipt wraps Vec<u8>.
ProcessingReceipt::new(bytes) constructs from a byte vector.
ProcessingReceipt::as_bytes() returns a &[u8] reference.
Debug output shows byte length, not raw bytes (e.g. "ProcessingReceipt(42 bytes)").
Two receipts with identical bytes compare as equal.

requirements.title: Store receipt against idempotency key to complete cache entry
requirements.spec: WHEN a consumer provides a ProcessingReceipt for a message with an idempotency key, THE SYSTEM SHALL update the corresponding dedup cache entry to include the receipt. The update SHALL use DurableStore::append to store the receipt against the existing cache entry's stream key. IF the idempotency key does not exist in the cache, THE SYSTEM SHALL return DurabilityError::DedupCollision. The receipt completes the dedup cycle: future duplicate publishes with this key will receive the stored receipt.
acceptance: complete_receipt(key, receipt) stores the receipt in the cache entry.
After completion, a duplicate publish with the same key returns the stored receipt.
Completing a receipt for a non-existent key returns DurabilityError::DedupCollision.
The receipt is stored via DurableStore::append, not by mutating the original entry.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement durable channel append logic — that is DUR-002.
SHALL NOT implement consumer cursor tracking — that is DUR-003.
SHALL NOT implement conversation event sourcing — that is DUR-005.
SHALL NOT implement crash recovery orchestration — that is DUR-006.
SHALL NOT interpret receipt contents — receipts are opaque bytes stored and returned verbatim.
SHALL NOT hardcode the dedup TTL or sweep interval — both are caller-configured.
verification: cargo check compiles dedup and receipt modules with no errors.
Test: duplicate key with receipt returns stored receipt without re-delivery.
Test: duplicate key without receipt returns DedupInFlight.
Test: sweep respects 1-second clock-skew margin (entry at TTL boundary is not removed).
Test: ProcessingReceipt round-trips through store and compare-equals.

adrs.title: Two exactly-once strategies: lightweight dedup contract and heavyweight durable conversations
adrs.decision: Two complementary strategies available per-channel. Lightweight: three-party dedup contract (producer idempotency key with TTL, bus deduplicates, consumer provides processing receipt). Heavyweight: durable conversations (consumer processing state is event-sourced, re-delivery triggers resume not re-execution — idempotency is emergent from resumable processing). The rejected alternative is a single exactly-once strategy (either too heavy for simple consumers or insufficient for complex ones).

adrs.title: Haematite (prolly tree) as the storage backend, not a general-purpose database
adrs.decision: Haematite is the sole storage backend. Content-addressed prolly tree with WAL buffer gives us branchable state, structural merge, hash-based sync, and actor-per-shard write parallelism — all properties that directly benefit the bus (conversation state branching, multi-node sync, zero-contention writes). The rejected alternative is pluggable backends (splits testing surface, dilutes the branchable-state differentiator, and adds abstraction overhead for a benefit that doesn't exist yet).
adrs.decided_by: Bono + Frodo + Sir Patrick Stewart convergence

checklist.text: Dedup cache stores idempotency key to processing receipt mapping in haematite via append(dedup_namespace + key_hash, receipt, expected_seq).
Duplicate publish with an existing idempotency key that has a receipt returns the stored receipt without re-delivering the message.
Duplicate publish with an existing idempotency key that has no receipt yet (processing in-flight) defers delivery per the channel's backpressure policy.
Background sweep removes dedup entries older than the configured TTL.
TTL expiry never removes an entry younger than the configured TTL, accounting for up to 1 second of clock skew.
Processing receipt is an opaque bytes value provided by the consumer after successful processing.
Receipt is stored against the idempotency key in the dedup cache, making the entry complete.

stories.text: As an application developer, I want to configure the dedup TTL per channel so that idempotency key storage matches my expected retry window without unbounded growth.
As an application developer, I want to provide a processing receipt after handling a dedup-protected message so that duplicate deliveries return my original result instead of re-executing.
As an operator, I want to see the dedup cache size and TTL expiry rate so that I can tune the dedup window to balance memory usage against duplicate protection.

constraints.text: No file over 500 lines. If a module approaches this limit, extract into submodules.
No panics in production code. .unwrap() and .expect() only in tests.
mod.rs contains ONLY pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. No logic, no type definitions, no function bodies.
All public types implement Debug. All error types implement std::error::Error via thiserror.
Every haematite append call must include an expected_seq for optimistic concurrency. No blind appends.
Consumer cursor checkpoint must use cas (compare-and-swap), never unconditional write. A stale checkpoint must fail, not silently regress.
Durable conversation replay must be idempotent: replaying the same event log twice produces identical state.
Ephemeral channels must not hold a reference to the DurableStore. The type system must prevent accidental persistence calls on ephemeral channels.
Dedup TTL expiry must never delete a receipt that is younger than the configured TTL, even under clock skew up to 1 second.
All durability operations must propagate haematite errors as DurabilityError variants, never swallow or log-and-continue.

resolved_context.intention:
When this cluster is complete, a developer can declare a channel as durable and get message persistence, replay from offset, and crash recovery without changing their publish/subscribe code. A developer building complex processing can choose a durable conversation and get exactly-once semantics that are emergent from resumable processing — not from delivery guarantees and not from application-level idempotency boilerplate.

The durability layer should feel like a channel property, not a separate system. Setting a channel to durable adds persistence; setting it to ephemeral bypasses haematite entirely with zero overhead. The two exactly-once strategies (lightweight dedup contract and heavyweight durable conversations) are complementary tools chosen per-channel, not competing approaches forced on all consumers.

The bus's relationship with haematite is direct and mechanical: append for writes, cas for cursors, read for replay. No ORM, no query builder, no abstraction that hides what haematite is doing. Developers who understand haematite's append/read/cas model understand the durability layer.

provenance.quote: When this cluster is complete, a developer can declare a channel as durable and get message persistence, replay from offset, and crash recovery without changing their publish/subscribe code. A develop
